|Sydney|New South Wales|Australia: Sydney, New South Wales.  Population: 5,450,496.  Area: 12,145 km².  Sydney is the capital city of the state of New South Wales, and is the most populous city in Australia. Located on Australia's east coast, the metropolis surrounds Sydney Harbour and extends about 80 kilometres (50 mi) from the Pacific Ocean in the east to the Blue Mountains in the west, and about 80 kilometres (50 mi) from Ku-ring-gai Chase National Park and the Hawkesbury River in the north and north-west, to the Royal National Park and Macarthur in the south and south-west. Greater Sydney consists of 658 suburbs, spread across 33 local government areas.
ocean|Tasman Sea: Tasman Sea.  The Tasman Sea is a marginal sea of the South Pacific Ocean, situated between Australia and New Zealand. It measures about 2,000 km (1,200 mi) across and about 2,800 km (1,700 mi) from north to south. The sea was named after the Dutch explorer Abel Janszoon Tasman, who in 1642 was the first known European to cross it.
|Blackwater|Queensland|Australia: Blackwater, Queensland.  Population: 4,702 (last census 2021).  Blackwater is a rural town and locality in the Central Highlands Region, Queensland, Australia. It is within a significant coal mining area in Central Queensland. The name of the township was inspired by the dark colour of local waterholes.
ocean|Pacific Ocean: Pacific Ocean.  The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest of Earth's five oceanic divisions. It stretches from the Arctic Ocean in the north to the Southern Ocean, or, depending on the definition, to Antarctica in the south, and is bounded by the continents of Asia and Australia in the west and the Americas in the east.
|Al Ghurayfah|Wādī al Ḩayāt|Libya: Libya.  Population: 7,381,023.  Area: 1.8 million km².  Libya, officially the State of Libya, is a country in the Maghreb region of North Africa. It borders the Mediterranean Sea to the north, Egypt to the east, Sudan to the southeast, Chad to the south, Niger to the southwest, Algeria to the west, and Tunisia to the northwest. With an area of almost 1.8 million km2 (700,000 sq mi), Libya is the fourth-largest country in Africa and the Arab world, and the 16th-largest in the world.
Cleveland|Cleveland|Queensland|Australia: Cleveland, Queensland.  Population: 15,850 (last census 2021).  Cleveland is a coastal and central locality in the City of Redland, Queensland, Australia. In the 2021 census, Cleveland had a population of 15,850 people.
|Cleveland|Queensland|Australia: Cleveland, Queensland.  Population: 15,850 (last census 2021).  Cleveland is a coastal and central locality in the City of Redland, Queensland, Australia. In the 2021 census, Cleveland had a population of 15,850 people.
|Toogoolawah|Queensland|Australia: Toogoolawah, Queensland.  Population: 1,200 (last census 2021).  Toogoolawah is a rural town and locality in the Somerset Region, Queensland, Australia. In the 2021 census, Toogoolawah had a population of 1,200 people.
Moore|Moore|Queensland|Australia: Moore, Queensland.  Population: 286 (last census 2021).  Moore is a rural town and locality in the Somerset Region, Queensland, Australia. In the 2021 census, the locality of Moore had a population of 286 people.
Julia Creek|Julia Creek|Queensland|Austr: Julia Creek, Queensland.  Population: 549 (last census 2021).  Julia Creek is an outback town and locality in the Shire of Mckinlay, Queensland, Australia. In the 2021 census, the locality of Julia Creek had a population of 549 people.
|Aurukun|Queensland|Australia: Aurukun, Queensland.  Population: 1,101 (last census 2021).  Aurukun is a town and coastal locality in the Shire of Aurukun and the Shire of Cook in Far North Queensland, Australia. It is an Indigenous community. In the 2021 census, the locality of Aurukun had a population of 1,101 people, of whom 997 (88.7%) identified as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander people.
|Mission River|Queensland|Australia: Mission River, Queensland.  Population: 974 (last census 2021).  Mission River is a coastal rural locality split between the Shire of Cook and the Aboriginal Shire of Napranum in Queensland, Australia. In the 2021 census, Mission River had a population of 974 people.
||South Australia|Australia: South Australia Australian rules football team.  The South Australia state football team is the representative side of South Australia in the sport of Australian rules football.
|Nhill|Victoria|Australia:
Nhill, Victoria.  Population: 2,401 (last census 2021).  Nhill is a town in the Wimmera, in western Victoria, Australia. Nhill is located on the Western Highway, halfway between Adelaide and Melbourne. At the 2016 census, Nhill had a
population of 1,749.
